title: actinella tweediei
intro: Actinella tweediei, also known as Tweedie's snuff box or actinella, is a species of air-breathing land snail that belongs to the family Camaenidae and is primarily found in Australia. This plant is not actually a plant but a type of snail. However, the Actinella tweediei snail has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments. The health benefits of Actinella tweediei are still largely anecdotal, but it is said to have anti-inflammatory properties and may be used to treat conditions such as arthritis, with herbal preparations including infusions made from the snail's shell and body.
